title: rosa pimpinellifolia var. myriacantha
intro: Rosa pimpinellifolia var. myriacantha, also known as the Burnet rose, is a plant species in the rose family (Rosaceae) with a specific variety characterized by its distinctive myriacantha foliage and fragrant flowers. The plant's leaves and flowers have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, which can help to soothe and calm irritated skin and mucous membranes. Rosa pimpinellifolia var. myriacantha is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties. The plant can be prepared as an herbal infusion, tea, or tincture, and its flowers can also be used to make a fragrant and calming oil or salve.
